pi_items.source: Derived from the event name '<text>2024 Noto Peninsula, Japan Earthquake</text>' in data/fdsn/noto_earthquake.xml.
Task instruction description explicitly requires setting certainty to 'known'.
noto_earthquake.xml does not provide certainty and convert_quakeml.py does not set it, meaning the DB column typeCertainty is NULL, which scolv displays as 'not set'.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real USGS Earthquake Catalog data for the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake (event ID us6000m0xl, magnitude 7.5). The SeisComP database initially has a NULL type certainty because the source QuakeML does not define it, appearing as 'not set' in the scolv UI. The agent is explicitly required to launch scolv and change the certainty to 'known'.
